tasks.title: Fix agent not investigating TOML parse errors in CI output
tasks.description: The RQ-0124 agent saw the error 'TOML parse error at line 44, column 18: unknown variant py314' multiple times but never investigated the pyproject.toml file. The error clearly pointed to line 44 of pyproject.toml with the invalid value 'py314'. The agent should have read the file and fixed the target-version. This is an agent behavior improvement - when CI fails with a parse error pointing to a specific file and line, investigate that file.
evidence: log: TOML parse error at line 44, column 18: unknown variant `py314`
log: Agent ran `make ci 2>&1 | tail -30` multiple times but never investigated pyproject.toml
file: ~/Projects/.workspaces/cloop/parallel/RQ-0124/pyproject.toml:44 contains target-version = "py314"
code: crates/ralph/src/commands/run/supervision/ci.rs already has error pattern detection with guidance
code: compliance messages include DETECTED ERROR section with file, line, invalid value, valid options
plan: Add CI Error Investigation section to ~/.codex/AGENTS.md
Pattern: 'parse error at line N' -> READ the file at line N immediately
Pattern: 'unknown variant X, expected Y' -> replace X with valid option from Y
Pattern: Error mentions specific file -> investigate that file first
Rationale: Ralph's CI gate already extracts file/line/invalid-value but agents ignore it
notes: Scope changed from ~/.claude/AGENTS.md (doesn't exist) to ~/.codex/AGENTS.md (actual global config)
No code changes needed - Ralph already provides structured error detection in compliance messages
Added 'CI Error Investigation' section to global AGENTS.md (~/.codex/AGENTS.md)
Guidance covers parse error patterns and required investigation actions
Includes concrete py314 example from RQ-0124 incident
Project-local AGENTS.md not modified per 'do not repeat global guidance' rule

tasks.title: Add CI output passthrough to agent before compliance message
tasks.description: When CI gate fails, the agent receives a compliance message but may not see the full CI output. In RQ-0124, the agent kept running `make ci` manually and truncating output with `tail -30`. The CI gate failure log showed the ruff parse error at the start of output (before tests). Agent should see the complete CI output including format-check, lint-check phases that run before tests.
evidence: log: Agent ran `make ci 2>&1 | tail -30` which truncated early errors
log: ruff format --check runs BEFORE tests, error appeared at start of CI output
analysis: Agent needs to see full CI output when gate fails
code: format_ci_output_for_message() takes LAST N lines, truncating early errors
code: detect_ci_error_pattern() scans combined output but agent only sees truncated snippet
plan: Change format_ci_output_for_message to show BOTH start and end of output
First 50 lines (early errors like format/lint) + last 50 lines (test failures)
Include line count indicator when truncated (e.g., 'Lines 1-50 of 200')
Add unit tests for head+tail truncation behavior
Run cargo test -p ralph to verify
notes: Modified format_ci_output_for_message() to show both head (early errors) and tail (test failures) instead of just tail
Changed function signature from max_lines to max_head_lines + max_tail_lines (50, 50)
Added 2 new tests: format_ci_output_shows_head_and_tail and format_ci_output_shows_all_when_small
All 40 CI supervision tests pass, full CI gate passes

tasks.title: Improve CI gate logging with full command output
tasks.description: The CI gate logs show 'CI gate (make ci): start' and 'error: CI failed' but don't include the actual command output. The full output appears elsewhere in the agent's log but not in the CI gate's own logging. This makes it harder to debug CI gate failures. Include the truncated command output in the CI gate log entry.
evidence: log: [2026-02-18T22:11:01Z INFO] CI gate (make ci): start
log: [2026-02-18T22:11:01Z ERROR] CI gate (make ci): error: CI failed
analysis: No command output in CI gate log entries
code: run_ci_gate() returns Ok(CiGateResult) even on CI failure - doesn't trigger with_scope error path
code: log::error! calls at lines 453-461 log output but separate from scope logging
plan: Create new with_scope_output helper in logging.rs for scopes that capture output
Modify run_ci_gate to return Result<CiGateResult, CiGateError> where error includes truncated output
Include detected error pattern type in log message
Add unit tests for the new logging behavior
Run cargo test -p ralph --lib -- supervision::ci to verify
notes: Root cause: run_ci_gate returns Ok(CiGateResult{success: false}) instead of Err, so with_scope logs 'end' not 'error'
Current output logging at lines 453-461 is good but disconnected from scope boundary
Solution: Make CI failure return Err with rich output context so with_scope can log it properly
Added CiFailure error type with Display impl including exit code, error pattern, truncated stdout/stderr
run_ci_gate now returns Err(CiFailure) on CI failure for proper with_scope logging
Updated call sites in mod.rs and parallel_worker.rs to use if let Err pattern
Added 5 new tests for CiFailure display behavior
All 1963 tests pass, CI gate passes

tasks.title: Add webhook queue overflow handling to parallel workers
tasks.description: The log shows multiple 'Webhook enqueue failed (queue full or worker disconnected)' warnings. Webhooks are used for progress reporting but fail silently in parallel worker mode. This could lead to lost status updates. Either increase queue size for parallel mode or implement webhook retry/overflow handling.
evidence: log: [2026-02-18T20:27:53Z WARN] Webhook enqueue failed (queue full or worker disconnected)
log: Multiple occurrences throughout parallel run
code: webhook/mod.rs:205-214 :: Default queue capacity is 100, configurable 10-10000
code: webhook/mod.rs:388-449 :: Three backpressure policies exist (DropOldest, DropNew, BlockWithTimeout)
code: orchestration.rs:279-283 :: Loop-level webhooks sent without checking queue capacity
code: orchestration.rs:845-853, 1061-1069 :: notify_loop_stopped called on completion paths
analysis: With N parallel workers each sending 4+ webhooks (phase_started/completed x2), queue of 100 fills rapidly during slow webhook delivery (30s timeout x 3 retries = 90s worst case per webhook)
plan: Increase default queue capacity from 100 to 500 for better parallel mode handling
Scale queue capacity dynamically based on parallel worker count (workers * 50 minimum)
Add parallel_mode_queue_capacity config option under agent.webhook
Log dropped webhook events with task_id and event type for debugging
Consider non-blocking batch enqueue for parallel mode webhooks
Run `cargo test -p ralph --lib -- webhook` to verify existing tests pass
Run `make macos-ci` to verify no regressions
notes: Root cause: Single global webhook worker thread with bounded queue (default 100) cannot keep up with burst of webhooks from N parallel workers
Each parallel worker sends: loop_started, phase_started (P1), phase_completed (P1), phase_started (P2/3), phase_completed (P2/3), loop_stopped = ~6 webhooks per task
With 8 workers, that's 48 webhooks minimum per wave. During slow delivery (90s per webhook), queue fills rapidly
Current policies (DropOldest, DropNew, BlockWithTimeout) all result in dropped messages under load
Webhooks are best-effort per module docs, but losing all status updates is poor UX
Increased default webhook queue capacity from 100 to 500
Added parallel_queue_multiplier config option (range 1.0-10.0, default 2.0)
Added init_worker_for_parallel function for parallel-aware capacity scaling
Improved dropped message logging to include event type and task_id
Phase 3 review: implementation complete and correct, all tests pass

tasks.title: Add SIGTERM signal handler to daemon serve
tasks.description: The daemon serve function does not register any signal handlers for SIGTERM. The only shutdown mechanism is via the stop signal file. If the daemon receives SIGTERM (e.g., from system shutdown or kill without -9), it won't clean up gracefully - the state file and lock will remain. Files: crates/ralph/src/commands/daemon/serve.rs.
evidence: path: crates/ralph/src/commands/daemon/serve.rs :: entire file :: no SIGTERM handler
analysis: On SIGTERM, daemon exits without cleanup, leaving state file and lock
plan: Add signal handler registration for SIGTERM in serve.rs
Use signal_hook or tokio::signal for cross-platform support
On SIGTERM, trigger graceful shutdown (clean state file, release lock)
Run `cargo test -p ralph` to verify

tasks.title: Add overflow protection to backoff computation

plan: Add overflow check: cap backoff at Duration::MAX

Add tests for edge cases

tasks.title: Add Ctrl-C check during backoff sleep in retry logic
tasks.description: The Ctrl-C check in runutil/execution.rs happens AFTER the sleep completes, not during it. If Ctrl-C is pressed during sleep, the code waits for the full backoff duration before checking.
evidence: path: crates/ralph/src/runutil/execution.rs :: lines 410-420 :: Ctrl-C check after sleep
plan: Replace thread::sleep with interruptible wait
Check Ctrl-C at shorter intervals during backoff
Add test for quick Ctrl-C response
Run `cargo test -p ralph` to verify

tasks.title: Add tests for JSON parsing edge cases in streaming
tasks.description: The parse_json_line function silently discards malformed lines without error. The fallback parsing only looks for { which would miss JSON arrays starting with [.
evidence: path: crates/ralph/src/runner/execution/json.rs :: lines 5-24 :: no distinction between empty and malformed
plan: Add tests for empty, valid object, valid array, malformed cases
Consider logging when malformed JSON is discarded
Run `cargo test -p ralph` to verify

tasks.title: Fix deny_unknown_fields blocking migration of deprecated config keys
tasks.description: ConfigLayer uses deny_unknown_fields which means if a user has a config with a deprecated field, the config will fail to parse BEFORE migrations can run. The migration system cannot help because parsing fails first.
evidence: path: crates/ralph/src/config/layer.rs :: line 30 :: deny_unknown_fields
analysis: Users must manually remove deprecated fields before upgrading
plan: Evaluate impact: list deprecated config keys
Option 1: Remove deny_unknown_fields
Option 2: Run migrations before full config parsing
Add test for deprecated key migration
Run `cargo test -p ralph` to verify

tasks.title: Fix Makefile test target race condition
tasks.description: The `make test` target has a race condition where cleanup runs before the error handler can cat the log file. The `trap cleanup EXIT` causes cleanup to run before error branch's cat, removing the log file.
evidence: path: Makefile :: lines 63-96 :: test target with trap cleanup EXIT
command: make macos-ci :: cat: .../unit-tests.log: No such file or directory
plan: Refactor to capture log content before cleanup
Option 1: Read log into variable before exit
Option 2: Use separate trap for ERR vs EXIT
Run `make macos-ci` to verify

custom_fields.stale_makefile_context: RQ-0942 centralized env reset in Makefile; test target now uses $(RALPH_ENV_RESET) and evidence line ranges should be refreshed before implementing this task.
